body_txt: When you're a celebrity, or become one overnight, tabloid stories will turn your personal life into a trashy melodrama. Try not to take it too seriously, even if they are pronouncing nuptials for you and some celebrity you merely had a friendly conversation with. Some tabloids start from a grain of truth and then spin a tale from it. For example, paparazzi could snap a photo of a celebrity leaving a drug clinic, and from this, they speculate that the person has an addiction (although in truth they may have simply been visiting a pal). Some tabloids and gossip sites may make stuff up, although this makes them liable to lawsuits. Even if the tabloids' claims are false , they can cause real life harm, as the celebrity may lose an upcoming appearance or job, or their marriage may be affected. A common subject of the tabloids are the dating lives of the rich and famous so expect to see the Celebrity Power Couple and stories about Normie Dates a Celebrity featured. Truth in Television (but not in tabloids), especially applied to pop stars and Hollywood actresses and actors. For characters who work for such publications, see Paparazzi , the aggressive photographers who hound celebrities. Also see Lurid Tales of Doom .
